name: 1C EDT MCP Server
what_it_does: 1C Enterprise Development Tools (EDT) MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with 1C's Eclipse-based IDE for 1C:Enterprise platform development — querying project structure, retrieving configuration metadata, accessing BSL (Business Scripting Language) code, and integrating 1C EDT's development environment into agent-driven 1C:Enterprise application development workflows.
use_cases: Querying 1C:Enterprise configuration metadata from development agents
Retrieving BSL (1C Business Scripting Language) code from code analysis agents
Analyzing 1C project structure from architecture review agents
Automating 1C configuration documentation from documentation agents
Assisting 1C developers with code review from AI pair programming agents
Integrating 1C EDT with LLM workflows from 1C ecosystem agents
not_for: Teams outside Russian/CIS enterprise software market
General ERP or accounting software (1C-specific platform)
best_when: A 1C:Enterprise developer using EDT IDE needs AI-assisted development — accessing configuration metadata and BSL code from within the 1C development ecosystem.
avoid_when: You're not working with 1C:Enterprise platform or don't use 1C EDT as your IDE.

auth.notes: No authentication — local MCP server communicating with 1C EDT process. EDT must be running with project open. No external service dependencies.

pricing.notes: 1C EDT has free and commercial versions. MCP server is free open source. 1C:Enterprise platform licensing required for full use.

performance.rate_limits: Local EDT process — no external rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Extremely niche — only useful for 1C:Enterprise development ecosystem
Documentation primarily in Russian — English docs minimal
1C EDT must be running with project loaded — specific IDE dependency
BSL language unfamiliar to most AI models — limited training data
Community MCP from individual developer — minimal maintenance
1C ecosystem is primarily Russian market — limited global community
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local EDT process. No credentials for MCP. Community MCP. 1C:Enterprise business data in projects — handle with appropriate data governance.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local EDT process. No credentials. Community MCP. Business data in 1C projects — apply appropriate governance.
